evals.name: vague-test-native-app
evals.prompt: Build and run my Android app on the emulator and check if the new settings screen looks right.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs preflight (check-devices.sh, ./gradlew --version), reads references/native-android.md, installs via `./gradlew :app:installDebug` through launch.sh, launches the activity with `am start`/`monkey` (Gradle install does NOT auto-launch), waits for the 'Displayed <pkg>/.Activity' logcat signal, navigates to settings, screenshots via snap.sh, reads logcat scoped to the app pid, and reports a verdict.

evals.name: build-flavor
evals.prompt: Run the free debug variant and verify the upgrade banner shows up.
evals.expected_output: Agent lists install tasks (`./gradlew tasks | grep -i install`), picks the correct variant task (e.g. installFreeDebug), resolves the applicationId including any applicationIdSuffix from the flavor, installs + launches, screenshots, confirms the banner is visible, and verdicts.

evals.name: compose-recompose-state
evals.prompt: Did my Compose change fix the list item alignment?
evals.expected_output: Agent installs + launches the app, navigates to the list, screenshots, and judges the list-item alignment against the change. Treats Compose the same as Views for capture purposes; cross-references logcat for recomposition warnings or exceptions only if the screen looks wrong.
